canto_title: The Limbo and the Great Hall of Heroes

scenes.summary: Dante is jolted awake by a heavy sound and begins to survey his surroundings.
Dante describes the dark, deep, and misty landscape of the abyss.
Virgil instructs Dante to follow him into the dark world, prompting Dante's fear of being left alone.
Virgil explains the sorrow of the unbaptized as they enter the first circle of Hell.
Dante observes the vast multitude of men, women, and children who exist in eternal sighing.
Virgil explains that these souls are not punished for sin, but lack the grace of baptism.
Deeply saddened by the news, Dante asks if any soul has ever been saved from this state.
Virgil recounts how great figures like Moses, Abraham, and David were brought to salvation.
The poets continue their journey through a thick forest of spirits.
A bright light appears in the distance, illuminating a place inhabited by honorable souls.
An unseen voice calls out to honor the great poet Virgil.

scenes.scene_name: The Meeting of the Great Poets
scenes.summary: Four great classical poets—Homer, Horace, Ovid, and Lucan—approach the travelers.
Dante is honored to be included in the company of these great minds.
The travelers reach a magnificent, seven-walled castle surrounded by a stream.
Passing through the gates, they enter a bright, open meadow filled with solemn souls.
Dante identifies legendary figures from Troy, Rome, and Greek myth.
Dante sees the great masters of philosophy, science, and medicine seated together.
Dante confesses he cannot name all the spirits because the subject is too vast.
Virgil leads Dante away from the quiet assembly toward a place of greater light.
